command_examples: If config file is required these locations will be searched for config.yml: "." "/etc/s3scanner/" "$HOME/.s3scanner/"

documentation.readme: Features - Usage - Quick Start - Installation - Discuss
usage_sections: Features: ⚡️ Multi-threaded scanning 🔭 Supports many built-in S3 storage providers or custom 🕵️‍♀️ Scans all bucket permissions to find misconfigurations 💾 Save results to Postgres database 🐇 Connect to RabbitMQ for automated scanning at scale 🐳 Docker support
Quick Start: Scan AWS for bucket names listed in a file, enumerate all objects Scan a bucket in GCP, enumerate all objects, and save results to database

arguments.name: -bucket
arguments.description: string Name of bucket to check.

arguments.name: -bucket-file
arguments.description: string File of bucket names to check.

arguments.name: -db
arguments.description: Save results to a Postgres database. Requires config file key "db.uri". Default: "false"

arguments.name: -enumerate
arguments.description: Enumerate bucket objects (can be time-consuming). Default: "false"

arguments.name: -json
arguments.description: Print logs to stdout in JSON format instead of human-readable. Default: "false"

arguments.name: -mq
arguments.description: Connect to RabbitMQ to get buckets. Requires config file key "mq". Default: "false"

arguments.name: -provider
arguments.description: string Object storage provider: aws, custom, digitalocean, dreamhost, gcp, linode, scaleway - custom requires config file. Default: "aws"

arguments.name: -threads
arguments.description: int Number of threads to scan with. Default: "4"

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

examples: If config file is required these locations will be searched for config.yml: "." "/etc/s3scanner/" "$HOME/.s3scanner/"

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the recon category. Fits when the engagement needs: - AWS - DigitalOcean - DreamHost - GCP - Linode - Scaleway - Custom.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Features - Usage - Quick Start - Installation - Discuss.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `If config file is required these locations will be searched for config.yml: "." "/etc/s3scanner/" "$HOME/.s3scanner/"`. Further invocations from upstream: `s3scanner -bucket-file names.txt -enumerate`; `s3scanner -provider gcp -db -bucket my-bucket -enumerate`. - AWS - DigitalOcean - DreamHost - GCP - Linode - Scaleway - Custom.

name: S3Scanner
summary: - AWS - DigitalOcean - DreamHost - GCP - Linode - Scaleway - Custom

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need recon, use S3Scanner because - AWS - DigitalOcean - DreamHost - GCP - Linode - Scaleway - Custom.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `If config file is required these locations will be searched for config.yml: "." "/etc/s3scanner/" "$HOME/.s3scanner/"` as documented upstream for S3Scanner.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `s3scanner -bucket-file names.txt -enumerate` as documented upstream for S3Scanner.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `s3scanner -provider gcp -db -bucket my-bucket -enumerate` as documented upstream for S3Scanner.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `s3scanner -bucket secret_uploads` as documented upstream for S3Scanner.
When chaining recon tools, pair S3Scanner with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `S3Scanner --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
